title: Japanese Braised Pork Belly
description: A rich and flavorful Japanese-inspired dish featuring tender braised pork belly in a savory fermented soy sauce-based sauce.
instructions: Season the pork belly with Chinese five-spice powder.
In a large Dutch oven, heat sesame oil over medium-high heat. Sear the pork belly until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add garlic and ginger to the pot and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add fermented soy sauce, soy sauce, and brown sugar to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the pork belly to the pot and bring the sauce to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the pork for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Slice the pork belly into thick slices and place it on a serving platter.
Drizzle the braising sauce over the pork and garnish with chopped green onions.
